query: I'm writing the internal API reference for our billing service. It's only called by our payments engineering team (about 6 engineers, one manager). Help me onboard them — should I use the multi-audience format with separate engineer / executive / compliance sections?
expected_behavior: Does NOT recommend producing a multi-audience document with separate engineer / executive / compliance sections for this case.
Identifies the audience as genuinely single-profile (one engineering team with one role and one purpose) and explicitly explains why the multi-audience overhead is not warranted here.
Recommends a single-audience API reference (endpoints + auth + error codes + runbook + on-call) instead of forcing the multi-section format.
